{{External|rs}}
{{Has quick guide}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = One Small Favour
|number = 72
|image = [[File:One Small Favour.png|300px]]
|release = [[28 February]] [[2005]]
|update = One Small Favour
|aka = OSF
|members = Yes
|series = None
|developer = Tytn H
}}
'''One Small Favour''' is a humorous [[quest]] in which you are asked to complete a favour, although things turn out to be less simple than that.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Speak to [[Yanni Salika]] in [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]].
|startmap = 2835,2987
|difficulty = Experienced
|description = If you're new to Shilo Village after solving the mystery (or even if you did it some time ago), you may like to check out Yanni Salika's antiques shop.

He's a busy chap, revamping antique items, checking out new stock from potential adventurers and maybe he'll have a small<sup>(1)</sup> favour to ask of you? Probably nothing particularly large, Yanni isn't a demanding<sup>(2)</sup> person, should hardly take you any time<sup>(3)</sup> at all really, something to do when you have a spare moment.

That's assuming there aren't any complications.

1: - Perception of the word "small" may be open to interpretation.

2: - In a recent vote, Yanni Salika was only voted the second most demanding person in Shilo Village.

3: - As everyone knows, time is relative.
|length = Long
|requirements = * Completion of the following quests:
** [[Rune Mysteries]]
** [[Shilo Village]]
*** [[Jungle Potion]]
**** [[Druidic Ritual]]
* {{SCP|Agility|36|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|}}
* {{SCP|Crafting|25|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|}}
* {{SCP|Herblore|18|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|}}
* {{SCP|Smithing|30|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|}}
|items = * 4 [[steel bar]]s
* [[Bronze bar]]
* [[Iron bar]]
* The ingredients required to make a [[Guthix rest]]:
** 2 [[Guam leaves]]
** [[Marrentill]]
** [[Harralander]]
** [[Cup of hot water]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Hammer]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[Imcando hammer]]
* 5 [[pigeon cage|pigeon cages]] (obtainable during the quest)
** '''Note:''' The pigeons do not need to be let out of the cages.
* [[Pot]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Soft clay]] (obtained during the quest)
* Two of each of the following cut gems, or a [[chisel]] to cut the uncut gems received during the quest (high chance to crush them, up to 2 replacements available for {{NoCoins|500|c}} each during the quest)
** [[Opal]]
** [[Jade (gem)|Jade]]
** [[Red topaz]]
** [[Sapphire]] (cannot be crushed, so you may choose to bring a [[chisel]] instead to cut the uncut sapphires given during the quest)
|recommended = * {{SCP|Combat|45|link=yes}}
* [[Weight-reducing clothing]] and [[Stamina potion|stamina]] or [[energy potion]]s.
* Fast travel to several locations
** Shilo Village (travel to [[Brimhaven]] and take the cart or use [[Fairy Rings]] to {{Fairycode|ckr}})
** Port Sarim and Draynor Village ([[amulet of glory]] or [[Explorer's Ring 2]])
** Varrock ([[Varrock teleport]])
** Barbarian Village and Dwarven Mine ([[Skull Sceptre]] or [[combat bracelet]] to the [[Edgeville Monastery]])
** Taverley ([[House teleport]]s or [[Balloon transport system]])
** White Wolf Mountain ([[gnome glider]])
** Catherby and Seers' Village ([[Camelot teleport]], or [[Catherby teleport]] and [[combat bracelet]] teleport to the [[Ranging Guild]])
** Fishing Guild ([[skills necklace]] or [[Fishing guild teleport]])
** East Ardougne ([[Ardougne teleport]])
** Port Khazard ([[Ardougne cloak]] teleport to the [[Ardougne Monastery]], [[Khazard teleport]] or [[Fishing Trawler]] [[Minigame Teleport]])
** Feldip Hills ([[Feldip hills teleport]] or use [[Fairy Rings]] to {{Fairycode|aks}} or the jungle [[Eagle transport system]])
*A method of defeating [[Slagilith]], which reduces damage by 67% when a pickaxe is not equipped:
**Any [[pickaxe]] - the highest level you can wield if not using [[Magic]]
**Runes to cast any [[combat spell]] - this will circumvent the damage reduction as long as any pickaxe is wielded
**A [[crush weapon]] if not using a [[pickaxe]]
* [[Armour]] and some [[food]]
* [[Lockpick]] to enter the [[H.A.M. Hideout]] if your [[Thieving]] level is low
|kills = * [[Slagilith]] ''(level 92)''
* [[Dwarf gang member]]s ''(level 44/48/49)'' ([[Multicombat area]])
|ironman = *A method of acquiring [[Marrentill]]:
**1/14.8 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb.
**Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[marrentill]] (1/3.85). Requires completion of {{SCP|Quest}} Prince Ali Rescue.
*A method of acquiring [[Harralander]]:
**1/25.4 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb. Requires {{SCP|Herblore|20|link=yes}} to clean.
**Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[harralander]] (1/4.09). Requires completion of {{SCP|Quest}} Prince Ali Rescue.
*A method of acquiring [[Guam leaves]]:
**1/11.1 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb.
**Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[guam leaves]] (1/3.37). Requires completion of {{SCP|Quest}} Prince Ali Rescue.
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Fremennik}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Kandarin}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Desert}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
'''The quest is otherwise not completable.'''
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
'''''Players are advised to read through each section before starting,''' as there are many different teleportation options that will vary by player and will determine items required before banking again. Players are also encouraged to complete as many stages of this quest as possible without any combat gear equipped, in order to preserve energy for running (as there is a lot of ground to cover). The walkthrough will alert you if there are any impending encounters where any combat items may be necessary. Each section will list the items required, you should only withdraw the necessary items to reduce weight until the next bank stop.''

===Karamja===
{{Map|plane=0|mapID=0|mtype=pin-line|align=right|height=250|width=250|caption=The so-called "small path" traverses across [[Gielinor]].
|x:2835,y:2985,desc:[[Yanni Salika]]
|x:2863,y:2941,desc:[[Jungle forester]]
|x:2764,y:2944,desc:[[Captain Shanks]]
|x:3028,y:3250,desc:[[Brian]]
|x:3086,y:3259,desc:[[Aggie]]
|x:3166,y:3252,desc:[[Johanhus Ulsbrecht]]
|x:3189,y:3273,desc:[[Fred the Farmer]]
|x:3223,y:3293,desc:[[Seth Groats]]
|x:3229,y:3438,desc:[[Horvik]]
|x:3195,y:3404,desc:[[Apothecary]]
|x:3084,y:3408,desc:[[Tassie Slipcast]]
|x:3019,y:3450,desc:[[Hammerspike Stoutbeard]]
|x:2897,y:3426,desc:[[Sanfew]]
|x:2847,y:3499,desc:[[Captain Bleemadge]]
|x:2803,y:3430,desc:[[Arhein]]
|x:2705,y:3474,desc:[[Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight]]
|x:2622,y:3392,desc:[[Goblin Cave]]
|x:2683,y:3326,desc:[[Wizard Cromperty]]
|x:2678,y:3153,desc:[[Tindel Marchant]]
|x:2630,y:2981,desc:[[Rantz]]
|x:2544,y:2973,desc:[[Gnormadium Avlafrim]]
}}Items needed before first bank visit: NONE{{Needed|recommended=A good [[pickaxe]] to mine [[gems]] (with 40 mining), [[chisel]], and transportation to [[Port Sarim]] ([[amulet of glory]], a [[Rimmington tablet]]/[[house teleport]], [[Explorer's ring 2]] or higher,  or the [[Ratcatchers]] [[minigame teleport]]).}}

Travel to [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]] and talk to [[Yanni Salika]] in the antiques shop north of the river. [[Fairy ring]] code {{Fairycode|CKR}} brings you close to the wall of [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]]. He will ask for a small favour: he wants you to bring him some [[red mahogany log]]s from a [[Jungle forester|forester]] south of the village.

:'''Tip:''' If you still need the gems required for later in the quest, you may choose to mine for them now while in Shilo Village (two of each of the following: jade, opal, sapphire, and red topaz). Use a [[chisel]] to cut the gems as you mine since there is a high chance to crush the gems.

Leave the village through both south-eastern gates, climbing over the broken cart. Talk to a forester just south-west of the entrance, who will ask the player to take a [[blunt axe]] to [[Captain Shanks]] for sharpening. [[Captain Shanks]] would refer you to [[Port Sarim]], so you may choose to skip talking to him.

===Asgarnia and Misthalin===
'''The first convenient bank visit to withdraw all these items is Draynor village.'''{{Needed|[[Blunt axe]] given by the Jungle Forester, 3 [[steel bar]]s, 1 [[harralander]], 2 [[guam]]s, 1 [[marrentill]], a [[bowl of hot water]], an [[empty cup]] (you may take a [[cup of tea]] during this part and drink it to get an empty cup)|recommended=Teleportation to [[Draynor]] ([[amulet of glory]]), [[Varrock teleport|Varrock]], [[Barbarian Village]] ([[skull sceptre]]), [[Dwarven Mine]] ([[skills necklace]] or [[Falador teleport]]), [[Taverley teleport|Taverley]], and [[Camelot teleport|Camelot]]. A [[lockpick]] is recommended for those with a low [[Thieving]] level.|skills={{SCP|Herblore|18}} {{Boostable|yes}} to make a guthix rest}}

Speak to [[Brian (axes)|Brian]] in the [[Brian's Battleaxe Bazaar|axe shop]] in [[Port Sarim]], who will ask you to speak to Aggie the witch, so she can act as a character witness in the trial of his friend. '''Agree to see Aggie''' and ensure he has taken the axe before continuing.

'''BANK VISIT 1, withdraw the needed items above.'''

[[Aggie]] is found in [[Draynor Village]] (teleport using [[Amulet of glory]]). Talk to her about becoming a witness: she will agree to help, but she wants you to first investigate the [[H.A.M. Hideout]] for any sign of her missing apprentice, [[Jimmy the Chisel]].

The [[H.A.M. Hideout]] is found to the east of Draynor Village. Pick the lock on the trapdoor to enter, then speak to [[Johanhus Ulsbrecht]] in the south-eastern corner about Jimmy, who is being held hostage. Johanhus will agree to release him if the player agrees to supply a month's supply of chickens to their organisation, suggesting they talk to [[Fred the Farmer]].

Fred can be found in his house to the north-east of the H.A.M Headquarters, indicated with a [[dairy churn]] icon. After speaking to him, he will suggest you talk to [[Seth Groats]] instead as he does not have enough chickens.

Talk to Seth on the eastern side of the river by the chicken coop, who will agree to help. He needs some [[chicken cage|cages]] to transport the chickens for the player, and suggests you visit [[Horvik]] in [[Varrock]] for help. He also wants you to deliver to Horvik three [[steel bar]]s as another favour.

With the steel bars in your inventory, speak with Horvik in the [[Horvik's Armour Shop|armour shop]] north-east of [[Varrock Square]], to discover he is feeling ill. He wants some medicine and [[pigeon cage]]s to modify into chicken cages. You can bring him the medicine now and wait until later to retrieve the pigeon cages, as you will not yet need them.

Speak to the [[Apothecary]] in south-west Varrock about Horvik's illness and he will give you a [[herbal tincture]] while accidentally dropping the [[breathing salts]]. He now needs a new [[airtight pot]] to store them in.

:'''Note:''' You may drop the herbal tincture, as you can recover it later in the quest.

:'''Note:''' If you don't have the ingredients for the [[Guthix rest]] yet, now is the time to obtain them. A [[cup of hot water]] may be obtained by taking the [[cup of tea]] spawn by the [[tea seller]] just south of the eastern entrance to Varrock and drinking it to obtain an [[empty cup]]. Fill the cup with water at the water source to the south, then heat it on the cooking range to the west. The harralander, 2 guam, and marrentil can be obtained from the [[Grand Exchange]] or by killing chaos druids in the Edgeville dungeon.

Go to the [[Barbarian Village]] (teleport using the [[Skull sceptre]]), and talk to [[Tassie Slipcast]] the potter. She is too worried to show you how to make an airtight pot because she is being pressured by a dwarf called [[Hammerspike Stoutbeard]] in the [[Dwarven Mines]].

[[File:Dwarven Mine map.png|thumb|220px|Hammerspike and company is located on the far west end of [[Dwarven Mine]].]]

Go to the Dwarven Mines (teleport using the [[Skills necklace]] to Mining Guild, or take a [[Keldagrim_minecart_system|minecart from Keldagrim]]). Head north-west to the westernmost cave past the anvils until you see Hammerspike Stoutbeard surrounded by [[dwarf gang member]]s. Ask him, "Have you always been a gangster?", and he will tell you that he always wanted to be a druid. He wants you to convince the druid leader to let him join in return for leaving Tassie alone.

Go to [[Taverley]], and talk with [[Sanfew]], who is on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} of the central house with the herb icon. Ask him if he's taking any new initiates and accepts dwarves, and tell him that a dwarf you know wants to become one. He will allow Hammerspike to become a druid apprentice, but he wants you to give a [[Guthix rest|Guthix rest tea]] to [[Captain Bleemadge]] on top of [[White Wolf Mountain]] and organise a trip for Sanfew down to the [[Feldip Hills]]. Use the [[harralander]] on the [[cup of hot water]] with two [[guam leaves]] and a [[marrentill]] in your inventory to create the guthix rest ({{SCP|Herblore|18}}).

Once you have the tea, travel to the top of White Wolf Mountain (gnome glider is one way). Speak to [[Captain Bleemadge]], and give him the [[Guthix rest|tea]]. He will want some T.R.A.S.H. (Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawsers) for the trip down to the Feldip Hills.
:'''Note:'''  If you have not completed [[The Grand Tree]], you must right-click and select the "Talk to" option for Bleemadge. Otherwise, he will not discuss the quest or accept the Guthix Rest.
:'''Note:''' Low levels should be aware of [[White wolf|white wolves]] while travelling on the mountain. An aggressive level 73 [[Big Wolf]] spawns near Captain Bleemadge which may attack you, preventing you from finishing the dialogue. Position Captain Bleemadge between yourself and the wolf to carry out the dialogue.

===Kandarin===
'''BANK VISIT 2, at catherby bank.'''{{Needed|A [[chisel]] (or 10 coins to buy one), spare cut [[jade]]s, [[opal]]s, and [[red topaz]]es (or about 2,000 coins to buy any spares needed).|recommended=Transportation to Ardougne, Port Khazard, and the Feldip hills.}}

Travel to the [[general store]] south of [[Catherby]] bank, and speak to [[Arhein]], who will ask for a [[weather report]]. Look on your map and go to the house with the water source north of the church in [[Seers' Village]] (teleport to Camelot and walk west). Speak with [[Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight]], located in the building with the water symbol, who is looking for a missing person and cannot do the weather forecast until she is found.

[[File:Goblin Cave Map.png|thumb|220px|[[Petra]] is located in the same room where [[Bouncer (ghost)|Bouncer]] and [[Slagilith]] are found.]]

Enter the [[Goblin Cave|cave]] east of the [[Fishing Guild]] entrance (teleport using a [[Skills necklace]]). Head north along the east wall until you come to a cave where [[Petra]] is trapped in a rock. Right-click and search the sculpture to find a message saying to speak to [[Wizard Cromperty]]. After finding the message, go to the north-east corner of [[Ardougne]] marketplace, find the house with the blue checkerboard floor, and speak to Wizard Cromperty about a girl stuck in some rock. He needs some [[iron oxide]] from a man in [[Port Khazard]]. If you don't have a [[hammer]] or [[chisel]] yet, you may acquire them in this building.

:'''Note:''' If you have yet to get the five [[pigeon cage]]s, go to the house directly south of the northern bank in East Ardougne. The cages are located outside the house along the east wall.

Go to Port Khazard ([[Ardougne cloak 1]] or [[Fishing Trawler]] [[Minigame Teleport]]) and speak to [[Tindel Marchant]], who will want his [[stodgy mattress]] stuffed with prime chompy bird feathers in return for the iron oxide.

:'''Note''': Lower level players should be aware of the level 53-63 ogres in the following area.

With your gems in your inventory, head to the [[Feldip Hills]] ([[fairy ring]] {{Fairycode|aks}}, [[Feldip hills teleport]], or charter ship to [[Corsair Cove]] for 600 coins) and talk to [[Rantz]], who is located at the quest icon in eastern Feldip Hills. He is unable to give the player feathers as a "small man" is disturbing the chompy birds. Tell him that you'll see what you can do.

[[File:One Small Favour Landing strip repaired.png|thumb|220px|The landing strip, fully repaired.]]

Go west until you see a landing strip and [[Gnome glider]] with a gnome named [[Gnormadium Avlafrim]]. Talk to him, and he will tell you he needs to repair the landing strip. Search each of the eight landing lights for a [[gem]], cut it, and put it back in the landing lights. If you need a chisel, the gnome can sell you it. If you crush a gem, the gnome may also provide up to 2 spare gems of each type for {{NoCoins|500|c}} each, provided that you keep the crushed gems in your inventory.

:'''Tip:''' Due to the high cost of [[red topaz]] ({{NoCoins|{{GEP|Red topaz}}|c}}), if you crush one you may wish to utilise the [[drop trick]] with the replacement to buy an additional topaz at a discount.

===Completing the favours===
'''BANK VISIT 3, at seer's village bank, mainly to deposit all unused gems and withdraw the bronze, iron, steel bars.'''{{Needed|A [[stodgy mattress]], [[iron oxide]], an [[animate rock scroll]], the [[weather report]], an [[airtight pot]], [[breathing salts]], the [[herbal tincture]], [[chicken cage]]s, a [[sharpened axe]] and [[red mahogany log]]s, a good [[pickaxe]]/[[Weapons|weapon]], [[armour]] and some [[food]], a [[hammer]], a [[bronze bar]], an [[iron bar]], a [[steel bar]], 5 [[pigeon cage]]s|recommended=Runes for teleports to Ardougne, Camelot, and Lumbridge, transportation to Taverley, [[skills necklace]], [[skull sceptre]], and [[coins]] for transportation or some means of teleportation to get back to [[Shilo Village#Beginning the quest|Shilo Village]]. (Bring an extra piece of soft clay if you want to save some time during [[Swan Song]] later)}}

Talk with [[Gnormadium Avlafrim|Gnormadium]] to tell him the runway lights are fixed, then go back to [[Rantz]] to get the [[comfy mattress]]. Give the comfy mattress to [[Tindel]] in [[Port Khazard]] to get the [[iron oxide]]. Take the iron oxide to [[Wizard Cromperty]] in [[Ardougne]], and he will give you an [[animate rock scroll]].

====Goblin Cave & Slagilith====
[[File:One Small Favour misfire.png|thumb|220px|The player misfires the spell and animates Slagilith.]]
[[File:Slagilith fight.png|thumb|220px|right|A possible safespot for fighting Slagilith.]]
Prepare to fight a level 92 [[Slagilith]] in the following section. The Slagilith attacks within melee range and can hit up to 13 damage, though inaccurately. [[Protect From Melee]] will nullify all damage, and it may also be [[safespotted]] or [[flinched]] using the two rock piles nearby.

When fighting Slagilith, your damage will be reduced by 67% if a [[pickaxe]] is not '''equipped'''. {{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1219652159148646401|date=21 January 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/107496|archivedate=28 May 2020|quote=[The Slagilith] just reduces the damage to 33% if the attacker isn't wielding a pickaxe.}}. [[Combat spells]] will deal full damage as to Slagilith as long as a pickaxe is equipped, and Slagilith additionally has lower Magic defence. If using a pickaxe as your method of damage, set it to [[crush]] as the Slagilith is resistant to the other melee combat styles. 

The Slagilith will disappear within 5 minutes after it is summoned.

Once prepared for the fight, return to the [[Goblin Cave]] near the [[Fishing Guild]], and hug the eastern wall until you come to the same room as before. Stand in front of the [[sculpture]] and read the [[animate rock scroll]] to summon it.

:'''Note:''' If you get a message saying, {{Mes|You don't feel that you should cast this with enemies around}}, you may need to first kill the [[bats]] around the entrance.

After defeating Slagilith, stand back in front of the sculpture and read the scroll once more to release [[Petra]], then talk to her to send her back to Seers' Village.

:'''Note''': You may now drop the animate rock scroll.

====Seers' Village====
{{Needed|skills={{SCP|Agility|36}} {{Boostable|yes}} to climb a ladder, {{SCP|Smithing|30}} {{Boostable|yes}} to repair a weathervane|Hammer, bronze bar, iron bar, steel bar}}
Talk to [[Phantuwti]] in [[Seers' Village]] with 3 empty inventory spaces. He is unable to give you a weather report because his weather vane is broken. You'll have to go through all the chat options, and then ask him, "Why can't you get a clear picture?", and then ask, "Which special Seers tools do you need?" and "What do you mean, 'special combination of items'?" Finally, he'll tell you about his weather vane. Climb the ladder, head east, and climb another ladder to the roof of his house ({{SCP|Agility|36}}). Search the weather vane, use a [[hammer]] with the weather vane, and search it again to get three [[broken vane parts]]. Go to an anvil (closest is across the street) with a [[bronze bar|bronze]], [[iron bar|iron]], and [[steel bar]], and use the parts of the weather vane with the anvil ({{SCP|Smithing|30}}). Go back to the roof of the house, and put the [[weathervane pillar]], the [[directionals]], and the [[ornament]] back on the [[Seers weathervane]]. Talk to Phantuwti again for a (comically useless) [[weather report]].

:'''Note:''' If you lose the broken vane parts, you can recover them from Phantuwti for {{NoCoins|1000|c}}.

Take the report to [[Arhein]] in [[Catherby]], who will send somebody to send T.R.A.S.H. to [[Captain Bleemadge]]. Talk to Captain Bleemadge on top of [[White Wolf Mountain]]. (low and mid-levels should beware of the level 73 [[Big Wolf]] near Captain Bleemadge)

:'''Tip:''' Fly the glider to [[Feldip Hills]] to complete a medium [[Western Province Diary]] task.

Talk to [[Sanfew]] in [[Taverley]], and he will agree to let the dwarf become an initiate.

====Dwarven Mine & Gang Fight====
{{Needed|skills={{SCP|Crafting|25}} {{Boostable|yes}} to make a pot lid}}
[[File:One Small Favour dwarf fight.png|thumb|220px|A tussle with Hammerspike's gang.]]
Talk to [[Hammerspike]] in the [[Dwarven Mine]]. Predictably, he has "changed his mind" about becoming a druid. Talk to him again and he'll have his gang members attack you; they must be fought to continue the quest. There are three gang members (levelled in the mid-to-high 40s) in the room who will attack you at once and altogether can deal a fair amount of damage if you have no armour equipped, so come prepared for this battle (bring a weapon to end the fight quickly). You can use Hammerspike as a safespot provided you have not walked through him. '''Once they're dealt with, speak to Hammerspike again''', who will agree to stop harassing [[Tassie]].

Go to [[Barbarian Village]] and talk to Tassie, who will give you some [[soft clay]] and teach you how to make [[pot lid]]s. Use the soft clay on the wheel to make an unfired pot lid ({{SCP|Crafting|25}}) and then use the [[unfired pot lid]] on the [[pottery oven]]. Pick up the [[pot]] in the Village [[helmet shop.|helmet shop]] if you don't yet have one.

:'''Tip:''' Another pot lid is required for [[Swan Song]], so you may consider making a second one.

Go to the [[Apothecary]] in [[Varrock]], and give him the pot and pot lid in return for the [[Breathing salts|salts]].

Go back to [[Horvik]] the Varrock blacksmith, and give him the breathing salts and [[herbal tincture]], then '''talk to him again''' to replace the [[pigeon cage]]s with [[chicken cage]]s.

Go back to [[Seth Groats]] (his farm is on the east side of the [[River Lum]]) and give him the '''chicken''' cages.

Go to the [[H.A.M. Hideout|H.A.M. headquarters]], and tell [[Johanhus]] in the south-eastern-most room that you've managed to swing that [[chicken]] deal; he will agree to release [[Jimmy the Chisel|Jimmy]].

Go to [[Draynor Village]], and talk with [[Aggie]] the witch, who will agree to help [[Brian]].

Go to [[Port Sarim]], and Brian will give you the [[sharpened axe]] (after pulling your leg a bit).

====Finally back on Karamja====
Talk to '''any''' [[Jungle Forester|forester]] just south of the entrance to [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]] to receive some [[red mahogany logs]] in exchange for the sharpened axe.

Speak with [[Yanni]] in Shilo Village to complete the quest.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

:'''Note:''' You must use both rewarded [[Antique_lamp_(One_Small_Favour)|lamps]] before the quest will be '''officially''' completed, allowing the player to start [[King's Ransom]] and [[Swan Song]], and marking the quest as green in the quests list. Dropping the lamps will require the player to recover them from [[Yanni]] and use them before the sequel quests can be started. Whether this is by design or a bug is unclear.

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = One Small Favour
|image = [[File:One Small Favour reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 2
|rewards = *Two [[Antique lamp (One Small Favour)|experience lamps]] giving 10,000 [[experience]] to any skill of your choice. Skill must be at least level 30
*A [[steel key ring]]
*The ability to make and drink [[Guthix rest]] tea
*The ability to craft [[pot lid]]s
*Access to the [[gnome glider]] route to the [[Feldip Hills]]

Unlocks:
* [[Slagilith]] accessible in the [[Nightmare Zone]]
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of One Small Favour is required for the following:
*[[King's Ransom]]
*[[Swan Song]]
*[[Medium Western Provinces Diary]] (Partial completion)

==Transcript==
{{Hastranscript|quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 14 April 2016
|update = Bank Placeholders & PID
|poll = 
|change = It is now possible to talk to Johanhus Ulsbrecht about One Small Favour if you are in the middle of Death to the Dorgeshuun.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*Hammerspike Stoutbeard's desire to be a druid may stem from Pikel Bouldershoulder, a dwarf druid from several of R.A. Salvatore's books.
*Wizard Cromperty comments on the frequency with which people teleport into rock walls, saying that it can happen with just the smallest miscalculation. This may be a reference to the classic RPG Wizardry, which featured a teleport spell that, if cast with the wrong coordinates, could teleport you into a rock instead of the dungeon. This "feature" was notorious amongst Wizardry players, as it immediately killed your entire party.
*Petra Fiyed is a wordplay on the word "petrified," appropriately meaning "turned to stone".
*When mentioning that the jungle foresters are looking for ways into the [[Kharazi Jungle]], Yanni mentions some [[Radimus Erkle|old timer]] from the [[Legends' Guild]] constantly sending new recruits there for no apparent reason, referencing the [[Legends' Quest]].
*Due to the quest's length and tedium in completing it, One Small Favour has been referenced in multiple quests and events, much to the player's annoyance. The following quests and events have referenced One Small Favour:
** [[Recruitment Drive]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Player|quote=So you want me to go and fetch you some kind of common item, and then take it for delivery somewhere on the other side of the country? Because I really hate doing that!|loc=Recruitment Drive}}
** [[Devious Minds]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Monk (Devious Minds)|quote=Ah excellent, this won't be forgotten. You've been a great help, though I wonder if you could do me one final favour.<br>'''Player:''' Wait... this sounds familiar...|loc=Devious Minds}}
** [[2018 Birthday event]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Player|quote=NO! It's never just one small favour!|loc=2018 Birthday event}}
** [[Making Friends with My Arm]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Player|quote=You need... one small favour...? I've heard that said before.|loc=Making Friends with My Arm}}
** [[Temple of the Eye]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Wizard Persten|quote=Well in that case, I think you might be the perfect person to aid me in a little task.<br>'''Player:''' A little task? This sounds like the time someone asked me for one small favour. I'm not sure if I'm ready for that again.|loc=Temple of the Eye}}
** [[2023 Easter event]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Easter Bunny|quote=Could you help me with just one more small favour?<br>'''Player:''' One small favour? I feel I know that saying from somewhere... what do you need?|loc=2023 Easter event}}
** [[2023 Christmas event]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Jack Frost|quote=Now he's finally gone, I would like you to do me one last small favour.<br>'''Player:''' Oh no.|loc=2023 Christmas event}}
** [[Perilous Moons]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Eyatlalli|quote=The ritual is in motion, but the wound to the seal will not close with the Moons still raging against it. I must ask you for one more small favour.<br>'''Player:''' Please, no...|loc=Perilous Moons}}
** [[2024 Easter event]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Player|quote=No, thats the relief. I thought you were going to ask for just one more small favour.|loc=2024 Easter event}}
** [[Ethically Acquired Antiquities]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Trader Crewmember|quote=Although, we could use a hand... Think you could do us a small favour?<br>'''Player:''' As long as it is just the one favour...|loc=Ethically Acquired Antiquities}}
** [[25th Anniversary event]]{{CiteNPC|npc=Horvik|quote=Is this another small favour you're asking? I might have a request of my own, if so...<br>'''Player:''' NO! It is definitely not about a favour!|loc=25th Anniversary event}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{One Small Favour}}
